source.selection_rule: Bugs with Jira comments that explicitly describe a cause, root cause, or concrete reproduction mechanism
source.verification_method: Full Jira issue details were checked for each retained pattern: summary, status, resolution, and comments. Patterns are not treated as product truth unless supported by later Confluence, aligned Phoenix code, and OpenAPI (Swagger) evidence.

patterns.summary: Billing run stays In Progress for days

verified_source.comment_summary: Developer comment states that an incorrect condition caused main data preparation to fail and prevented the billing run from moving to Draft status.
verified_source.limitations: Not independently verified against code in this pattern file.

trigger_signature: Billing processing does not transition from In Progress to Draft
Main data preparation fails before expected fallback handling
patterns.repro_heuristic: Create billing data where invoice generation fails and verify state transition fallback handling.
patterns.validator_action: Use as a strong reproduction hypothesis for billing runs stuck in In Progress after a preparation failure.

patterns.summary: Request for disconnection includes same POD in multiple executed requests

verified_source.comment_summary: Comments provide explicit reproduce steps: keep first draft RFD open, execute second RFD with same PODs, then execute first RFD; backend now checks if PODs are used in another executed object.
verified_source.limitations: Exact endpoint/code path still needs cross-dependency or deep code/OpenAPI verification outside the minimal pattern text.

trigger_signature: Same POD appears in multiple executed disconnection requests
First draft object remains open while a second object is executed
Backend must reject the first execution after the second object already used the PODs
evidence_keywords: backend checks if PODs are used in another executed object
patterns.repro_heuristic: Create RFD draft with PODs, keep edit page open, execute another RFD with same PODs, then attempt to execute the first draft and expect a validation error.
patterns.validator_action: Use as a high-confidence pattern for race/edit-stale-state validation gaps.

patterns.summary: Real invoice converted from proforma is missing in VAT report

verified_source.comment_summary: Comment explicitly says the issue was caused by Current status change date not being updated when a paid real pro forma invoice is converted to a standard invoice; accounting period validation then excludes it.
verified_source.limitations: Needs code/reference verification when used for a new ticket.

trigger_signature: Real invoice exists but is excluded from VAT report
Conversion from proforma to standard invoice occurred
Invoice status date is older than accounting period opening date
evidence_keywords: caused by Current status change date
patterns.repro_heuristic: Convert paid proforma to standard invoice and validate report inclusion under accounting period date constraints.
patterns.validator_action: Use as a strong hypothesis when report inclusion depends on invoice status-date fields after conversion.

patterns.summary: Second contract version cannot be edited

verified_source.comment_summary: Comment states that the second contract version had an invalid entry-into-force date in DB, which caused the UI error; remediation was DB date correction.
verified_source.limitations: Status is Won't Do; treat as data-integrity pattern, not a confirmed application defect pattern.

trigger_signature: Edit action fails only for second contract version
Entry-into-force date contains malformed or impossible value
evidence_keywords: reason why error message appears in UI
patterns.repro_heuristic: Create or seed second contract version with invalid effective date format and attempt UI edit.
patterns.validator_action: Use only as a data-quality hypothesis; do not infer code defect unless Phoenix code and OpenAPI evidence confirms it.

patterns.summary: Price parameter remains blocked after canceled billing run

verified_source.comment_summary: Comment links this case to another issue and states that a billing run canceled before the fix left objects locked because generated profiles were not deleted after cancellation.
verified_source.limitations: Status is Won't Do and comment says the exact same issue cannot be reproduced after cleanup/fix; use only as historical cleanup/lock pattern.

trigger_signature: Objects remain locked after canceled billing run
Profiles generated by canceled billing are still present
evidence_keywords: after canceling billing run all objects were locked
patterns.repro_heuristic: Run billing, cancel before full completion, then verify whether generated profiles and locks are fully cleaned.
patterns.validator_action: Use as a candidate pattern for leftover locks after interrupted/canceled billing flows; require fresh reproduction.

patterns.summary: Mass blocking does not prevent reminder mass operation behavior

verified_source.comment_summary: Comment has an explicit Root cause section: ReminderProcessItemRepository.getLiabilitiesForReminderProcessing did not check mass operation blocking, so blocked customers were still included.
verified_source.limitations: Repository name is from Jira comment; code should still be checked when matching future bugs.

trigger_signature: Customers blocked by mass operation still processed for reminders
Reminder process query ignores block flag
Communication objects are generated for blocked customers
evidence_keywords: query ... didn't check mass operation blocking
patterns.repro_heuristic: Mass-block customers, execute reminder flow, and verify repository filtering includes block constraints.
patterns.validator_action: Use as high-confidence pattern for missing query predicates around blocking/date-range controls.

verified_source.comment_summary: Initial comment identifies scale code/type not selected in meters as the reason profile data was not generated. Later comment corrects details and says correction data by scales should use the same nomenclatures as base data by scale; final confirmation says invoice correction succeeded.
verified_source.limitations: Because an earlier explanation was corrected, use the generalized nomenclature/scale-mapping mismatch pattern rather than the first wording literally.

trigger_signature: Correction creates missing or incorrect profile data
Correction data by scales has nomenclature mismatch with base data or meter configuration
evidence_keywords: scale code and scale type is not selected in meters
reason why new data by profile is not generated
same nomenclatures as base data by scale
patterns.repro_heuristic: Prepare invoice correction where correction scale data does not align with base scale nomenclatures/meter configuration, then verify profile generation and debit note totals.
patterns.validator_action: Use as a controlled hypothesis for invoice correction mismatches; require exact nomenclature setup in test cases.

patterns.summary: Billing does not mark oldest profile data as invoiced
patterns.root_cause_type: Orphan SLP profiles without invoice linkage

verified_source.comment_summary: Comment confirms SLP profiles were generated without invoice_ids and therefore were not marked invoiced; exact reason for invoice generation failure is stated as possible period validation or something else.
verified_source.limitations: The missing-invoice reason is speculative; only the orphan-profile/no-invoice state is reliable.

trigger_signature: SLP profiles are generated but linked invoices are missing
Invoiced marker is not set on profile records
evidence_keywords: profile was generated from SLP flow
system did not generate an invoice
did not mark these profiles as invoiced
patterns.repro_heuristic: Generate SLP profiles in a flow where invoice creation can fail or be skipped, then verify whether profiles with missing invoice linkage are marked or cleaned correctly.
patterns.validator_action: Use only as a medium-confidence hypothesis; aligned Phoenix code and OpenAPI evidence must confirm the exact invoice-generation failure path.

patterns.summary: Invoice correction - PODs without correction are included in the credit and debit note
patterns.root_cause_type: DELETED billing_by_profile in correction staging with zero volume delta

verified_source.comment_summary: Prod run 3400: DELETED BBP mapped original=false in correction_billing_data_ids; ACTIVE vs DELETED SUM(value) identical; volume_change only; full_reversal_needed on correction_pods.

trigger_signature: INVOICE_CORRECTION volumeChange=true priceChange=false
billing_by_profile DELETED and invoiced=true on correction side
Zero volume delta between ACTIVE and DELETED profile per POD
correction_pods full_reversal_needed=true
patterns.repro_heuristic: Run PDT-2915 SQL checklist on correction billing_run_id; or Dev Playwright TO-BE spec after backend fix.
patterns.validator_action: High-confidence VALID pattern when queries 4+5 return DELETED original=false rows and zero_delta=true; use with Confluence correction-flow page 256114692.

validator_usage_notes.goal: Use these patterns to recover reproduce hypotheses when bug tickets are missing direct steps.
matching_strategy: Match by domain + evidence_keywords + trigger_signature overlap
Treat medium and medium_high patterns as candidate hypotheses requiring stronger code and OpenAPI (Swagger) evidence
Do not use Won't Do patterns as application-defect proof; use them as data-state or historical-flow hypotheses only
validator_usage_notes.verdict_policy: Pattern match improves reproduce-flow hypotheses but does not replace Confluence + aligned Phoenix code + OpenAPI (Swagger) evidence.
